briefing: Sessions on May 19 hit 37 -- up 85% versus the 28-day median of 20 and up 61% year-over-year from 23 -- a strong day worth understanding. The driver is direct traffic: the unpacked drill-down shows direct sessions jumped from a baseline of 9.5 to 26, accounting for nearly the entire lift. Pageviews followed at 142, up 91% versus median and 35% YoY, and new users doubled the median (28 vs. 14). Average session duration also rose to 143 seconds versus a median of 115, which suggests the people who came weren't bouncing -- they were reading. Nothing in the recent git commits points to a public content launch that would explain this; the recent activity is all internal operational tooling (the dashboard shipped May 18, commit 591d051) with no public-facing URLs attached. The efficacy rollup shows 8 pages ranking in the top 3 and 49 GA4 sessions attributed to the last 30 days of launches, though GSC impressions remain at zero across the board -- GSC data appears stale and should be treated as unreliable today. Today: check Camy's May 19 social posts or any email send that went out that day to see if the direct traffic spike traces back to a specific campaign or share.
